rulings.Case Title: LONA GUTIERREZ et al VS. AMCORD INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for October 6, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Amcord, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: TED HEBEBRAND ET AL VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for October 6, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 2. Plaintiffs' motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. rulings.Case Title: DANNY SHAW ET AL VS. JOHN CRANE INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for October 6, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed August 3, 2016 = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: DANNY SHAW ET AL VS. JOHN CRANE INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On Plaintiffs Claim For Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for October 6, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant John Crane Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from sustaining its burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of presenting evidence that conclusively negates an element of plaintiffs' punitive damages claim. Even if the Court considers all of defendant's evidence to which plaintiffs object, it is not sufficient to sustain defendant's burden. Defendant failed to demonstrate that public policy dictates that its motion should be granted. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
